steps.command: if [ -f .shelf-config ]; then cat .shelf-config; else echo 'No .shelf-config — using defaults'; echo "slug=$(basename $(git remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null | sed 's/.git$//') 2>/dev/null || basename $(pwd))"; echo 'base_path=projects'; fi

SHELF_PATH=$(jq -r '.plugins | to_entries[] | .value[] | select(.installPath | contains("/shelf/")) | .installPath' ~/.claude/plugins/installed_plugins.json 2>/dev/null | head -1); if [ -z "$SHELF_PATH" ]; then SHELF_PATH=plugin-shelf; fi; echo "shelf_plugin_path=$SHELF_PATH"

steps.instruction:
Read the current project dashboard from Obsidian and extract all sections.

You have MCP tools: mcp__obsidian-projects__read_file.

1. Read the shelf config output to get `base_path` and `slug`.
2. Read the dashboard: `mcp__obsidian-projects__read_file({ path: "{base_path}/{slug}/{slug}.md" })`
3. If not found: write "ERROR: No project found at {base_path}/{slug}/{slug}.md — run /shelf:create first" and stop.
4. Parse and output the dashboard content with sections clearly labeled:
   - FRONTMATTER: (the full YAML frontmatter)
   - TITLE: (the # heading)
   - DESCRIPTION: (text between title and first ## section)
   - HUMAN_NEEDED: (## Human Needed section content)
   - FEEDBACK: (## Feedback section content)
   - FEEDBACK_LOG: (## Feedback Log section content)
   - OTHER_SECTIONS: (any sections not matching the above)

Preserve the exact content of each section — do not modify or reformat.

Compare the existing dashboard to the current template and report structural differences.

1. Read the current template from context (read-current-template output).
2. Read the existing dashboard sections from context (read-existing-dashboard output).
3. Read the status labels from context (read-status-labels output) to check for non-canonical status values.
4. Compare and report:

## Status
- Current status value in frontmatter
- Whether it is canonical or non-canonical
- If non-canonical, what it should be normalized to

## Structural Changes
List each difference between the dashboard and template:
- Missing sections (sections in template but not in dashboard)
- Extra sections (sections in dashboard but not in template)
- Frontmatter field differences (missing fields, extra fields)
- Format differences (heading levels, section ordering)

## Content to Preserve
List user-written content that MUST be preserved during repair:
- Human Needed items (both checked and unchecked)
- Feedback entries
- Feedback Log entries
- Progress entries (if any exist inline)
- Description text

## Non-Canonical Labels Found
List any non-canonical status values and their canonical equivalents.

Write this report to your output. This report will be used by the apply-repairs step.

Apply template structure to the existing dashboard while preserving user content.

You have MCP tools: mcp__obsidian-projects__update_file.

1. Read all context:
   - read-shelf-config: get base_path, slug
   - read-current-template: the template structure to apply
   - read-existing-dashboard: the current dashboard content with labeled sections
   - generate-diff-report: what needs to change

2. Read the status labels from context (read-status-labels output) for canonical status values.

3. Rebuild the dashboard:
   a. Start with the template structure
   b. Populate frontmatter from existing values, but:
      - Normalize status to canonical value if non-canonical
      - Preserve all existing frontmatter fields (status, tags, next_step, last_updated, repo, project)
   c. Preserve the existing title and description
   d. Preserve ALL content from these sections exactly as-is:
      - ## Human Needed (all items, checked and unchecked)
      - ## Feedback (all entries)
      - ## Feedback Log (all entries)
   e. If the dashboard has sections not in the template, preserve them at the end

4. Write the rebuilt dashboard:
   `mcp__obsidian-projects__update_file({ path: "{base_path}/{slug}/{slug}.md", content: "{rebuilt dashboard}" })`

5. Report what was changed and what was preserved in your output.

IMPORTANT: Never delete user-written content. If in doubt, preserve it.

Re-read the dashboard after repair and confirm it matches the template structure.

You have MCP tools: mcp__obsidian-projects__read_file.

1. Read the shelf config to get base_path and slug.
2. Read the repaired dashboard: `mcp__obsidian-projects__read_file({ path: "{base_path}/{slug}/{slug}.md" })`
3. Read the template from context (read-current-template output).
4. Read the status labels from context (read-status-labels output) for canonical status validation.
5. Verify:
   - All template sections exist (## Human Needed, ## Feedback, ## Feedback Log)
   - Frontmatter has required fields (status, tags, next_step, last_updated, project)
   - Status is a canonical value
   - No template placeholders remain (no {slug}, {date}, etc.)
6. Report PASS or FAIL with details.

If FAIL, list specific issues found.
